prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for pyridine were computed at the DFT level using B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard'), PySCF RKS, exact four-centre integrals (no density fitting), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The cation (N−1) and anion (N+1) states were both doublets (multiplicity 2). Partial charges were computed via Mulliken (PySCF). The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied pyridine.xyz (no re-optimization at this level). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true; both Σf⁺ and Σf⁻ = 1.0000. No warnings were reported by the engine.

Headline result: the most electrophilic site is atom 1 (N) with f⁺ = 0.48422572; the most nucleophilic site is atom 4 (C) with f⁻ = 0.11826260. Full per-atom Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor) are tabulated in the preceding report.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
Output JSON: pyridine_fukui_dft.json
Bar chart: .../20260704-152935_pyridine_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
